question: Compare the NuScale RPV structural integrity program with conventional PWR vessel surveillance programs.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) editions as mandated by regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the inspection, shipment, installation, and surveillance requirements for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards for quality assurance and material testing. It specifies procedures for maintaining a dry, controlled environment during shipment and details where further information on inspections, operating conditions, and fasteners can be found. Key reference documents are listed at the end for compliance and technical guidance.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel over its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that predicted material properties remain within regulatory safety criteria throughout the vessel’s operational lifespan.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical barrier to fission product release and is constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime. Material selection for the RPV and its components prioritizes compliance with stringent standards for pressure retention and fracture toughness.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the use of ASME and NRC methodologies to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides these limits for various operational conditions to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using current instrumentation. Any leakage into containment, regardless of source, quickly vaporizes and disperses, so all leakage is treated as unidentified until specifically located and quantified by other means; overall leakage is monitored through RCS inventory balance and containment evacuation system (CES) flow rates, with operational limits set in technical specifications. The leakage detection systems are designed to be reliable, redundant, and sensitive enough to support leak-before-break safety analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) complies with relevant regulatory requirements, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria, by designing, fabricating, and testing reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Class 1 standards. It also clarifies the classification of connected piping and references the use of the 2013 Edition of the ASME BPVC, as endorsed by regulatory guides and rulemaking.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure boundary to prevent the release of fission products and provides structural support for various reactor components. Materials used for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design life.

edges.description: Section A describes the material selection and ASME code compliance for RPV threaded fasteners, while Section B outlines the inspection, shipment, and handling requirements for the RPV, both referencing ASME BPVC standards to ensure structural integrity.

Section A describes the selection and qualification of RPV threaded fastener materials in accordance with ASME and environmental compatibility requirements, while Section B lists references including EPRI chemistry guidelines and ASME quality assurance standards that underpin or inform the practices described in Section A.

Section A specifies the material selection and design standards for RPV threaded fasteners, which supports Section B's requirement that operating procedures maintain vessel integrity by ensuring pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, as both sections address complementary aspects of maintaining RPV structural integrity.
Section A details the materials and inspection requirements for RPV threaded fasteners, which are a subset of the broader RPV materials and structural components described in Section B.

Section A specifies that the RPV shipment, handling, and storage comply with ASME NQA-1-2008/1a-2009 Addenda, which is the same quality assurance standard referenced in Section B for the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary, indicating both programs rely on the same QA requirements.

Section A specifies the inspection, handling, and storage requirements for the NuScale RPV, while Section B describes the development of operating procedures to ensure vessel integrity under operational conditions, both of which are connected by their roles in maintaining RPV structural integrity as required by regulatory standards.

Section A specifies the inspection, handling, and storage requirements for the NuScale RPV, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV's structure and function, with Section A detailing procedures for the vessel described in Section B.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that inform the structural integrity program, while Section B describes the implementation of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity in compliance with technical specifications, thus Section A supports the regulatory and technical basis for the practices described in Section B.

Section A lists references, including industry guidelines and standards, that are relevant to the structural integrity and surveillance of reactor pressure vessels, while Section B describes the physical configuration and materials of the NuScale reactor vessel, thus Section A provides the regulatory and technical context that informs the design and surveillance approach detailed in Section B.

Section A describes the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which directly relies on the structural and material characteristics of the RPV detailed in Section B.

Section A discusses the examination and code compliance requirements for nonstructural attachments and stud welds on the RPV, while Section B details the material selection and code compliance for threaded fasteners used in the RPV, with both sections connected through their reliance on ASME BPVC requirements for ensuring RPV structural integrity.

Section A discusses specific ASME BPVC code requirements and examination practices for RPV attachments, while Section B references the broader inspection and handling requirements that include compliance with ASME BPVC and related standards, thus Section A provides details that specify how the general requirements in Section B are implemented.

Section A discusses specific ASME BPVC code requirements and examination practices for NuScale RPV components, while Section B lists references including ASME and EPRI documents that underpin or inform these requirements and practices.

Section A discusses the challenges of detecting and distinguishing RCS leakage in NuScale's design, while Section B details the material selection and inspection requirements for RPV fasteners, both addressing aspects of maintaining RPV structural integrity but focusing on different surveillance mechanisms.

Section A discusses the challenges of detecting reactor coolant pressure boundary leakage in NuScale's design, while Section B outlines the inspection and handling requirements for the reactor vessel, both of which contribute to the overall structural integrity program by addressing different aspects of vessel surveillance and integrity assurance.

Section A discusses the limitations of NuScale's leakage detection capabilities for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B lists references, including industry guidelines and standards, that may inform or support the technical basis for such detection programs.

Section A establishes that the NuScale RPV and its components, including threaded fasteners, comply with ASME BPVC Section III requirements, while Section B specifies that the threaded fasteners used in the RPV conform to these same ASME BPVC requirements, thus providing detailed material and design information that supports Section A's general compliance statement.

Section A establishes that the NuScale RPV complies with ASME BPVC Section III and regulatory requirements, while Section B provides specific details on how the RPV's shipment, installation, and environmental controls are performed in accordance with those same ASME code provisions.

Section A describes compliance with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements for RPV integrity, while Section B lists references (including ASME and EPRI documents) that provide the technical standards and guidelines supporting the compliance claims in Section A.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its role in the reactor coolant system, while Section B specifies the materials and inspection requirements for RPV threaded fasteners, detailing how structural integrity is maintained in accordance with regulatory codes.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B references specific inspection and handling requirements that apply to the RPV described in Section A.

Section A describes the physical configuration and role of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B lists references, including industry guidelines and standards, that underpin the integrity and surveillance requirements for reactor coolant boundary components like the RPV.

Section A describes the practical limitations of leakage detection in the NuScale RCS, while Section B asserts compliance with regulatory codes (10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC) that set requirements for RPV and pressure boundary integrity, connecting the operational detection capabilities to the overarching regulatory framework.

Section A discusses the detection of leakage from the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which includes the reactor pressure vessel described in Section B as a key component of the reactor coolant system.

Section A specifies that the NuScale RPV and associated components comply with regulatory and ASME Code requirements, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV's structure and its role in the reactor coolant system, thus Section A provides detailed compliance information for the general vessel described in Section B.
